title: Make Carbonite cron jobs PATH-safe
close_reason: Completed: cron setup now registers the executable Carbonite backup helper by absolute path, avoiding dependence on interactive shell PATH setup; deployment guide reflects the helper path.

title: Ignore QMD cache/model artifacts in Carbonite backups
close_reason: Completed: Carbonite now excludes QMD xdg-cache/xdg-config runtime cache, index, and model artifacts in generated gitignore and backup/init untracking paths; deployment guide documents post-restore rebuild expectations.

title: Investigate stale Carbonite backup lock after successful cron run
description: After the fresh rebuild/restore, sandbox-local Carbonite cron backups appear healthy (lastRunStatus ok and new backup commits exist), but manual carbonite-backup still reports "Another backup is already running. Skipping." even when no obvious backup/git/flock process is visible from a sandbox-side /proc probe. Investigate whether the lock is genuinely held by a lingering process, whether the sandbox lacks enough process-inspection tooling to see the holder, or whether the lock handling in carbonite-backup has a stale-holder edge case. Preserve current healthy backup behavior while diagnosing; do not blindly delete ~/.carbonite.lock without confirming the holder state.
notes: Confirmed root-cause clue from host-side /proc probe: the Carbonite lock was not held by an active backup, but by an orphaned interactive `/bin/bash -i` inside the sandbox (`PPid: 1`) with fd 9 open on `/sandbox/.carbonite.lock`. A normal `kill` did not clear it; `kill -9` on the holder PID was required before manual `carbonite-backup` could run again. After killing the holder, manual backup `post-carbonite fix backup` succeeded and pushed commit `a001d36`. This points to a likely OpenShell `sandbox connect` session-cleanup leak (user also observed unattended connect sessions ending with broken pipe), not a Carbonite backup logic failure by itself.

title: Preserve canonical memory continuity in backup/init flow
notes: Updated Carbonite backup/init contract to preserve canonical OpenClaw memory continuity from .openclaw-data/memory instead of the facade path under .openclaw/. The generated .gitignore now ignores the facade tree wholesale again, stage_preserved_paths in both scripts now includes .openclaw-data/memory, and the deployment guide now documents canonical memory preservation while keeping facade/runtime-integration paths out of scope. Extensions and secret-bearing env files remain excluded by design.
close_reason: Completed. Carbonite now preserves canonical OpenClaw memory continuity from .openclaw-data/memory while leaving facade paths, extensions, and secret-bearing runtime env files outside the backup contract. Scripts and deployment guide were updated, bash syntax checks passed, and the clawrbonite repo change was committed and pushed.
